name: Baked Apple Dumplings with Cranberry Filling
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
2. In a bowl, mix sour cream, granulated sugar, dried cranberries, and grated orange peel.
3. Core each apple, leaving the bottom intact and not peeling them.
4. Divide the pie pastry into six equal pieces.
5. Roll out one piece on a well-floured surface to form a 9-inch square.
6. Brush the dough lightly with water and place an apple in the center.
7. Fill the apple with the sour cream mixture.
8. Fold the sides of the pastry over the apple, twisting at the top to seal.
9. Cut off any excess pastry and set aside.
10. Press the seams against the apple to ensure a tight seal.
11. Refrigerate any remaining apples while preparing the rest.
12. Brush each dumpling with milk and sprinkle with 1 tsp granulated sugar.
13. Place the dumplings on a baking sheet, about 2 inches apart.
14. Prick the pastry several times with a fork, being careful not to pierce the apple skin.
15. If desired, roll out any excess dough and cut decorative leaves.
16. Attach the leaves to the dumplings using water.
17. Bake in the preheated oven for 30 minutes or until the apples are tender and the pastry is golden brown.
18. Serve warm.
